data.title: PAGE: Q4 2025 saw resilient US and Asia growth, but EMEA and UK declines led to a 4.6% profit drop
data.description:
Q4 2025 gross profit declined 4.6% year-over-year, with growth in the US and Asia Pacific offset by challenging conditions in EMEA and the UK. Cost optimisation delivered £5m in savings for 2025, with annualised savings of £15m expected from 2026.

Original document: PageGroup PLC [PAGE] Earnings Release — Jan. 13 2026

data.description:
A £6 billion healthcare REIT was formed through the Assura combination, delivering 60% of targeted synergies and strong rental growth. The group announced its 30th consecutive year of dividend increases, with robust liquidity and a positive outlook supported by government health initiatives.

data.title: Persimmon Expects Full Year Underlying PBT At Upper End Of Market Expectations
data.description:
Persimmon PLC :

  • PERSIMMON PLC - 2025 TRADING STATEMENT

  • PERSIMMON : FULL YEAR UNDERLYING PBT AT UPPER END OF MARKET EXPECTATIONS

  • PERSIMMON : IN 2025, TOTAL COMPLETIONS WERE UP 12% TO 11,905 HOMES

  • PERSIMMON : IN 2025, VALUE OF OUR FORWARD SALES POSITION INCREASED 2% TO £1.17BN

  • PERSIMMON : IN 2025, NET SALES RATE PER OUTLET PER WEEK EXCLUDING BULK SALES WAS UP 4% TO 0.59

  • PERSIMMON : PERFORMED WELL DURING 2025, IN A CHALLENGING MARKET

  • PERSIMMON : NOT EXPECTING ANY MATERIAL IMPROVEMENT IN MARKET CONDITIONS THIS YEAR

  • PERSIMMON : EXPECT FY UNDERLYING BUILD COST INFLATION TO BE SIMILAR TO 2025

  • PERSIMMON: FEWER BULK SALES IN ORDER BOOK, CONTINUED CHALLENGES IN REGISTERED PROVIDER MARKET, LIKELY TO SLOW GROWTH IN MARKETS IN 2026

data.title: B2 Impact Mulls Tap Issue Of EUR 100 Million
data.description:
B2 Impact ASA :

  • TAP ISSUE OF MINIMUM EUR 100 MILLION IN OUTSTANDING SENIOR UNSECURED BONDS DUE 18 JANUARY 2031 MAY FOLLOW

  • UNSECURED COLLECTION PERFORMANCE ABOVE 110% IN Q4 AND FULL YEAR

  • CASH EBITDA OF AROUND NOK 4.7 BILLION FOR FULL YEAR

  • LEVERAGE RATIO OF AROUND 2.1X AT END OF YEAR

  • INVESTMENTS WITHIN TARGET RANGE OF NOK 3.5 - 4 BILLION

  • WILL OFFER A CONDITIONAL BUY-BACK AT 103% OF PAR IN ITS OUTSTANDING BONDS DUE 18 MARCH 2029

data.title: SHI: Flat sales, improved profit, and strong cost savings amid weak demand and pricing pressure
data.description:
Flat like-for-like sales and improved operating profit were achieved despite subdued demand and pricing pressure. Cost reduction and restructuring drove significant savings, while liquidity and leverage remained stable. Vision 2030 aims for higher margins and robust cash generation.

Original document: SIG plc [SHI] Earnings Release — Jan. 13 2026

data.title: REG - Seascape Energy Asia - Long Term Incentive Plan Awards
data.description:
RNS Number : 5901O Seascape Energy Asia PLC 13 January 2026  

THIS ANNOUNCEMENT DOES NOT CONTAIN INSIDE INFORMATION FOR THE PURPOSES OF ARTICLE 7 OF REGULATION 596/2014 AS AMENDED AND TRANSPOSED INTO UK LAW IN ACCORDANCE WITH THE EUROPEAN UNION (WITHDRAWAL) ACT 2018 ("UK MAR").

13 January 2026

Seascape Energy Asia plc

(the "Company", "Seascape Energy" or "Seascape")

Notification of Transaction by Person Discharging Managerial Responsibilities ("PDMR")

Long Term Incentive Plan Awards

As part of the annual bonus cycle for the performance year ending 31 December 2025, the Executive Directors have elected to receive a significant proportion of their annual bonus in the form of  nil cost options to acquire ordinary shares of 10p each in the Company (the "LTIP Options") under the Company's Long Term Incentive Plan (the "LTIP"), to allow them to continue to build their equity stake in the Company and ensure alignment with shareholders.

In addition, Seascape announces the grant of an award under the Company's NED Plan (the "NED Plan") to Michael Buck, who has joined the Company's board of directors today as an Independent non-executive Director.

Following the vesting of the Awards and including existing shareholdings, the Directors will hold a total of 7.7% of the Company's issued share capital.

The LTIP

The LTIP, which is designed to provide incentivisation and retention for the Company's personnel, was approved by shareholders at the 2020 AGM and the principal terms are summarised in the Company's Re-admission document of 10 June 2021 (available on the Company's web site).

The Awards

The Company's Remuneration Committee, which is made up exclusively of Non-Executive Directors, has made awards under the LTIP to the following PDMRs (the "Awards") in the form of nil cost options to acquire ordinary shares of 10p each in the Company. The number of shares and percentage of the Company's current issued share capital ("ISC"), is set out below:

PDMR

Title

No. of Option shares

ISC%

Nick Ingrassia

CEO

232,721

0.37

James Menzies

Executive Chairman

164,991

0.26

Pierre Eliet

Executive Director

166,578

0.26

Julian Riddick

Company Secretary

51,982

0.08

The calculation of the Awards utilised an average market price of 64.7 pence per share and subject to the rules of the Plan the Options will vest in 12 months from the date of grant and be exercisable for a period of up to five years from grant.

The NED Plan Award

The NED Plan is designed to provide incentivisation and retention over a three-year period (the "Performance Period"), vesting from 13 January 2027 to 13 January 2029.  Following awards made to the other non-executive Directors and in the interests of consistency, the Company's Board of Directors has made an award under the NED Plan to Michael Buck in the form of market priced options to acquire ordinary shares of 10p each in the Company (the "NED Options").

The Board of Directors has used its discretion, as provided for under the NED Plan rules, to make an exceptional award (100% - 200%) equivalent to 110% of the annual fee payable for acting as a non-executive Director. The award has been sized to ensure that no judgement or performance is compromised and the number of shares and percentage of the Company's enlarged issued share capital ("ISC"), assuming full exercise of all the NED Options, is set out below:

PDMR

Michael Buck

Title

Independent Non-executive Director

61,300

0.1

No. of Options

ISC%

The NED Options have been set at price of 64.7 pence per share and subject to the rules of the Plan, the NED Options will vest in three equal annual tranches after grant and be exercisable for a period of up to five years from grant. 

Notification of Deal Forms of each PDMR can be found below. This announcement is made in accordance with Article 19 of the EU Market Abuse Regulation 596/2014.

Enquiries:

James Menzies (Executive Chairman)

Callum Stewart

Ashton Clanfield

SNELSeascape@Stifel.com

Cavendish Capital Markets Limited (Joint Broker)

Tel: +44 20 7397 8900

Neil McDonald

Pete Lynch

data.title: PSN: 12% completions growth and robust profit, but 2026 outlook cautious amid market headwinds
data.description:
Completions grew 12% and profit before tax is expected at the upper end of guidance, with strong cash generation and increased land investment. Outlook for 2026 is stable but cautious, with cost inflation and regulatory headwinds anticipated.

Original document: Persimmon Plc [PSN] Earnings Release — Jan. 13 2026

data.title: REG - Eurocell plc - Transaction in Own Shares
data.description:
RNS Number : 5788O Eurocell plc 13 January 2026  

EUROCELL PLC

("Eurocell" or the "Company")

Transaction in Own Shares

Eurocell announces that it has purchased (through Berenberg acting as the Company's

broker) the following number of its ordinary shares of 0.1p each on the London Stock Exchange.

Date of purchase:

12/01/2026

Number of ordinary shares purchased (aggregated volume):

14,579

Highest price paid per share (pence):

133.00

Lowest price paid per share (pence):

133.00

Volume weighted average price paid per share (pence):

133.00

Following the purchase/cancellation of these shares, the Company will have no shares held in treasury and the Company's issued share capital (excluding shares held as treasury shares) will consist of 99,735,277 shares.

The total number of voting rights in Eurocell is therefore 99,735,277. This figure may be used by shareholders as the denominator for the calculations by which they can determine if they are required to notify their interest, or a change to their interest, in the Company under the Financial Conduct Authority's Disclosure and Transparency Rules.

In accordance with Article 5(1)(b) of the UK version of Regulation (EU) No. 596/2014 which is part of UK law by virtue of the European Union (Withdrawal) Act 2018, the table below contains detailed information of the individual trades made by Berenberg as part of the Share Buyback Programme.
